AC: Corpus ingest reads ticket Summary/Body and optional doc paths unsupervised; candidate terms/phrases extracted from same corpus; dense embedder (ST default all-MiniLM-L6-v2, opt-in) encodes chunks and terms; semantic importance scores computed in vector space without human labels (document KeyBERT/centroid/discriminative method in docs); results stored in sqlite (term, score, source, embedder, optional cluster id); todo.py command surfaces top terms globally and per ticket; tests use mock/hash embedder for CI and optional ST integration test; SKILL/docs describe pipeline and explicitly distinguish from IDF/BM25 and from supervised fine-tuning.

Body.raw:
Build an unsupervised pipeline (not IDF/BM25, not supervised ST fine-tuning) that reads our documents and todo text, embeds chunks with sentence-transformers (or compatible dense embedder), and derives terms/phrases that are semantically important in vector space for our domain.

Context from design discussion:
- Hash embedder and lexical boosts are insufficient for semantic vocabulary discovery.
- sentence-transformers encode() is the engine only; importance scoring is our pipeline.
- NOT Apple Create ML / NLContextualEmbedding.
- NOT document-frequency IDF scores; importance means discriminative/salient in embedding geometry (e.g. term vectors aligned with doc/cluster centroids and far from global corpus centroid; KeyBERT-style doc-term cosine; cluster representatives after UMAP/HDBSCAN).

Relates to todo-embedding branch (sqlite + search) but this ticket is the separate semantic vocabulary layer: corpus ingest, candidate phrase mining, embed, score, persist term table, expose via todo.py (e.g. terms list / terms for doc / search boost hook).

Start after or alongside merging todo-embedding sqlite storage.

Summary.raw: Unsupervised semantic term vocabulary from corpus
